name: Column
description: Single freestanding column composed of three stacked elements: a flat @accent base course at y0 that flares wider than the shaft, a solid @primary shaft filling the central footprint from y0+1 to y_top-1, and a crowning capital at y_top whose geometry varies by style_variant (doric, ionic, corinthian). Used to mark thresholds, support porches, define strong centers in courtyards, or articulate the rhythm of a colonnade.

required_furniture.rule: Single @accent course at y0 that occupies the full AABB footprint, visibly wider than the shaft above (or a single @primary block when the footprint is 1x1).
Solid vertical Fill of @primary from y0+1 to y_top-1; for footprints >= 3-wide the shaft is inset by 1 cell so the base genuinely flares wider.

Crown at y_top: doric = flat @accent slab matching footprint; ionic = @accent slab + ring of @stairs facing outward on each side; corinthian = ionic capital + a second one-cell-inset @accent cap above when vertical room allows.

placement_rules: Footprint is square in plan; asymmetric AABB inputs are squared by taking the larger horizontal extent.
Stair-flared capitals (ionic, corinthian) only render on footprints >= 2-wide; thin 1x1 columns always get a single-block doric cap.
Capital stairs face outward from the column center on each edge cell.
lighting_ventilation: Columns are structural and do not light themselves; pair with adjacent @light skills (sconces, lanterns) when placing in dim interiors. The freestanding silhouette does not obstruct ventilation.

medieval.notes: Rough cobblestone shaft on a stone-brick base and capital — fits castles, abbeys, and great halls.

modern.notes: Quartz-pillar shaft (vertical grain) on a smooth-quartz base/capital — minimalist civic column.

fantasy.notes: Purpur-pillar shaft with purpur capital and base — End-themed column for sanctuaries and arcane towers.
